qa_pairs.question: How do you install the Apache bandwidth module (`mod_bw`) to limit bandwidth on a web server?
qa_pairs.answer: To install the Apache bandwidth module (`mod_bw`), first, run the command `sudo apt install libapache2-mod-bw`. After installation, enable the module using the command `sudo a2enmod bw`.

qa_pairs.question: How is the Apache bandwidth module (`mod_bw`) configured to limit total bandwidth from all clients to 100 Mbps?
qa_pairs.answer:
To limit total bandwidth from all clients to 100 Mbps, you need to add specific configuration directives within the `<VirtualHost></VirtualHost>` tags of your Apache site configuration. For instance, in the default Apache site configuration file, `/etc/apache2/sites-enabled/000-default.conf`, you would include:
```
BandWidthModule On
ForceBandWidthModule On
BandWidth all 12500000
```
Note that 100 Mbps translates to 12,500,000 bytes/s (100 * 1/8 * 1e6).
